message:
feat: exclusive restart-operation lock and structured concurrency test coverage

Restart-operation lock (main.rs):
- Add restart_operation_lock_path() to derive a .restart.lock file path
  from the session database path (e.g. /tmp/ft.db -> /tmp/ft.restart.lock).
- Add acquire_restart_operation_lock() using the existing WatcherLock
  mechanism to prevent concurrent snapshot restore or restart operations
  from racing on the same database.
- Wire the lock into both the `ft restart` path (in run()) and the
  `ft snapshot restore` path (in handle_snapshot_command()), with
  JSON and stderr error output depending on emit_json mode.
- Tests: lock path derivation and mutual exclusion (second acquire fails,
  re-acquire after drop succeeds).

Structured concurrency docs and tests (cx.rs):
- Expand module-level docs with migration guidance: spawn_with_cx for
  child work, spawn_bounded_with_cx for JoinSet-style fanout,
  spawn_with_timeout for compatibility seams.
- Add doc note on spawn_bounded_with_cx as the replacement for ad-hoc
  JoinSet or task-vector patterns.
- Test spawn_bounded_preserves_order_when_tasks_finish_out_of_order:
  verifies result vector maintains input order even when tasks complete
  in reverse order (30ms, 0ms, 10ms, 20ms delays).
- Test spawn_bounded_waits_for_all_children_before_returning: verifies
  the function does not return until all 4 children have incremented
  the atomic counter, even with concurrency limit 2.
- Test spawn_with_timeout_supports_nested_runtime_compat_spawn (behind
  asupersync-runtime feature): verifies nested runtime_compat::task::spawn
  works inside a timeout boundary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
